lesson: A LID OPEN alarm with the lid shut and the hall sensor reading zero field means the lid retention magnet has fallen out — an $8 part, not the board.
symptoms: LID OPEN alarm at every startup.

tests.error-log: LID OPEN fault every startup for two days.
tests.inspect-beater: Hopper lid and hinge look fine. The small retention magnet in the lid body has fallen out — hall sensor can't detect it.
tests.continuity-test: Hall effect sensor reads zero field. Magnet is missing.

flavour: Eight dollars of magnet was holding the entire operation hostage.
authenticityNote: Hopper lid magnets are small press-fit items that fall out if the lid is dropped or slammed. The hall sensor reads no field and locks the machine out — frequently misdiagnosed as a board fault.
